cases.description: SEARCH is case-insensitive: lowercase 'a' matches the leading uppercase 'A' -> position 1 (contrast with FIND on this exact same input, which is case-sensitive and returns #VALUE!)
SEARCH supports wildcards unlike FIND: '*' matches any sequence of characters, so "p*e" matches the substring "pple" starting at position 2
cases.expected_note: Source: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/search-function-9ab04538-0e55-4719-a72e-b6f54513b495 - SEARCH allows the wildcard characters '?' and '*' in find_text; FIND does not support wildcards at all

cases.description: '?' wildcard matches exactly one character, so "?pple" matches "Apple" starting at position 1
find_text not present anywhere in within_text -> #VALUE!
